title: 谋杀启事
summary:
Inspector Corradino asks Letitia Blacklock for a list of the inhabitants of her house. Miss Blacklock explains that, in addition to herself and Dora Bunner, the household includes two distant cousins, Patrick and Julia Simmons, who have been paying guests for two months. Patrick is a member of the navy, and Julia is a pharmacy trainee at Milchester General Hospital. Other residents and staff include Mrs. Hyems, a gardener who lives in a cottage with her eight-year-old son; Mrs. Higgins, who works five mornings a week; and a foreign refugee who serves as the cook. Miss Blacklock also mentions Miss Picker, whom she describes as a suspicious woman with a persecution complex who tells outrageous lies.

Corradino then interviews Julia Simmons regarding the shooting. Julia describes the evening as a series of visitors, including Captain Eastbrook, Miss Chinchcliff, Miss Muggleton, the Sweetnams, and Mrs. Harmon. She recounts how the lights went out and a masked figure entered, shooting Miss Blacklock twice. Julia notes that the man used a moving flashlight that blinded the guests. She observes that the event felt "staged," suggesting that if the killer had intended to harm Miss Blacklock secretly, they would not have chosen a public setting with many witnesses.

Following the interview, Corradino seeks out Micky in the kitchen with Fletcher's guidance. He finds Micky rolling dough, noting her somber appearance and suspicious demeanor.
